factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in the truck and parts segments.

evidence.text: Parts sales were a record $1.26 billion in 2021 compared to $1.02 billion in 2020 reflecting higher demand in all markets. Truck industry retail sales in the U.S. and Canada in 2021 are expected to be 230,000 to 250,000 units compared to 216,500 in 2020.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand, particularly in key segments, supported by record sales and increased unit expectations.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to higher revenues.

evidence.text: Average truck sales prices increased sales by $140.1 million primarily due to higher price realization.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices, contributing to higher revenues, as evidenced by increased sales figures.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Europe and South America.

evidence.text: Truck net sales and revenues in Europe increased by 31% in the first nine months of 2021. Mexico, South America, Australia and other regions saw a 57% increase in net sales and revenues.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue is shifting towards higher-margin regions, supported by significant increases in net sales and revenues.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating across all segments.

evidence.text: Worldwide net sales and revenues were $16.84 billion in 2021 compared to $13.16 billion in 2020, primarily due to higher truck and parts revenues.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with a substantial year-over-year increase in worldwide net sales and revenues.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across all markets, driven by real demand.

evidence.text: Truck deliveries increased in all markets in the first nine months of 2021 compared to 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across all markets, driven by real demand, as evidenced by increased deliveries.

factors.summary: The fastest-growing end markets are North America and Europe, with South America also showing strong growth.

evidence.text: Truck industry retail sales in the U.S. and Canada in 2021 are expected to be 230,000 to 250,000 units compared to 216,500 in 2020. In Europe, truck industry registrations for over 16-tonne vehicles are expected to be 260,000 to 280,000 units compared to 230,400 in 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The fastest-growing end markets show strong growth, supported by increased sales expectations in key regions.

factors.summary: Raw material and commodity costs are rising, but the company is managing through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Average cost per truck increased cost of sales by $147.3 million, primarily reflecting higher raw material costs, labor and freight.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising raw material and commodity costs are creating headwinds, despite efforts to manage through pricing and productivity.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure.

evidence.text: Factory overhead and other indirect costs increased $71.0 million primarily due to higher labor costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing labor costs are contributing to margin pressure, as evidenced by higher factory overhead and indirect costs.

factors.summary: Gross margins are compressing due to higher costs and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Truck gross margin was 5.7% in the third quarter of 2021 compared to 8.4% in the same period of 2020 due to the factors noted above.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are compressing due to higher costs and supply chain disruptions, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: The supply chain is still disrupted, particularly due to semiconductor shortages.

evidence.text: The Company has been affected by an industry-wide undersupply of semiconductor chips which reduced truck deliveries by approximately 7,000 vehicles in the third quarter of 2021 and resulted in approximately 10,500 trucks in inventory awaiting components at the end of the quarter.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The supply chain is disrupted due to semiconductor shortages, affecting truck deliveries and inventory.

factors.summary: Manufacturing utilization is increasing, reflecting higher demand.

evidence.text: Deliveries in the same period of 2020 reflect the lower build rates caused by lower demand partially as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Manufacturing utilization is increasing, reflecting higher demand, though evidence is somewhat limited.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, though there are still challenges with semiconductor shortages.

evidence.text: The Company anticipates the semiconductor shortage will continue to temper deliveries in the fourth quarter of the year and into 2022.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are normalizing, but ongoing semiconductor shortages pose challenges, leading to a mixed signal.

factors.summary: Management is confident, with increased capital investments and R&D spending.

evidence.text: Capital investments in 2021 are expected to be $525 to $550 million and R&D is expected to be $320 to $330 million. In 2022, capital investments are projected to be $425 to $475 million and R&D is expected to be $350 to $400 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident with increased capital investments and R&D spending, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing semiconductor shortages and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: The Company anticipates the semiconductor shortage will continue to temper deliveries in the fourth quarter of the year and into 2022.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include ongoing semiconductor shortages and supply chain disruptions, posing significant challenges.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on new technologies and market expansion.

evidence.text: The Company is investing to grow its business with new aerodynamic truck models, clean diesel and zero emissions powertrain technologies, advanced driver assistance systems, connected vehicle services and next-generation manufacturing and distribution capabilities.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction with significant investments in new technologies and market expansion.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include semiconductor shortages, raw material costs, and economic conditions.

evidence.text: The Company has been affected by an industry-wide undersupply of semiconductor chips which reduced truck deliveries by approximately 7,000 vehicles in the third quarter of 2021 and resulted in approximately 10,500 trucks in inventory awaiting components at the end of the quarter.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include semiconductor shortages, raw material costs, and economic conditions, indicating significant risks.

factors.summary: New product launches, such as electric and hydrogen fuel cell trucks, are key catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: Peterbilt delivered ten Model 579EV battery electric trucks in the third quarter of 2021 which will operate at the Port of Oakland and will deliver five more in the fourth quarter to the Port of Long Beach. Kenworth delivered ten T680E hydrogen fuel cell trucks to the Port of Los Angeles for field trials with partners Shell, Toyota and UPS.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches, such as electric and hydrogen fuel cell trucks, indicate growth opportunities.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing, supporting new product development.

evidence.text: R&D expenses were $72.5 million in 2021 compared to $64.7 million in 2020. In 2022, R&D is expected to be $350 to $400 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing R&D investment supports innovation and new product development.

factors.summary: New products, such as electric and hydrogen fuel cell trucks, are gaining traction.

evidence.text: Peterbilt delivered ten Model 579EV battery electric trucks in the third quarter of 2021 which will operate at the Port of Oakland and will deliver five more in the fourth quarter to the Port of Long Beach. Kenworth delivered ten T680E hydrogen fuel cell trucks to the Port of Los Angeles for field trials with partners Shell, Toyota and UPS.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful deliveries of new electric and hydrogen trucks show traction in the market.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable, with strong freight demand and high fleet utilization.

evidence.text: Current high levels of freight tonnage, freight rates and fleet utilization are contributing to customers’ profitability and cash flow.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable economic conditions with strong freight demand support growth.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are favorable, aiding revenue growth.

evidence.text: Favorable currency translation effects contributed to higher revenues in 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable foreign exchange effects contribute to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Capital investments in 2021 are expected to be $525 to $550 million and R&D is expected to be $320 to $330 million. In 2022, capital investments are projected to be $425 to $475 million and R&D is expected to be $350 to $400 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures signal confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Market share is stable or slightly declining in some regions.

evidence.text: The Company’s heavy-duty truck retail market share was 29.6% in the first nine months of 2021 compared to 29.7% in the first nine months of 2020. DAF over 16‑tonne market share was a 15.8% in the first nine months of 2021 compared to 16.2% in the same period of 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Market share is stable with minor regional declines, indicating no clear trend.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include new technologies and market expansion.

evidence.text: The Company is investing to grow its business with new aerodynamic truck models, clean diesel and zero emissions powertrain technologies, advanced driver assistance systems, connected vehicle services and next-generation manufacturing and distribution capabilities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investments in new technologies and market expansion enhance competitive position.
